stem: A agriculture study measures crop yield across 3 regions, each containing multiple farms. region A has 15 observations with mean 70.3 tonnes/hectare (within-region SD = 3.8). region B has 8 observations with mean 58.8 tonnes/hectare (within-region SD = 8.0). region C has 8 observations with mean 68.0 tonnes/hectare (within-region SD = 8.8). The between-region standard deviation is uncertain, estimated between 3.1 and 7.8 tonnes/hectare. Using a hierarchical Bayesian model, estimate the true crop yield for region B, accounting for shrinkage toward the grand mean and the uncertainty in the between-region variation.

ground_truth.answer: Hierarchical estimate for region B: 60.6 tonnes/hectare (shrunk from raw 58.8). Range [59.7, 62.4] as between-region SD varies from 3.1 to 7.8. Pooled mean: 66.7.

solution_steps: Compute pooled (grand) mean: 66.7 tonnes/hectare (weighted average of 3 region means).
Shrinkage factor for region B: B = σ²_between / (σ²_between + σ²_within/8). At σ_between=5.2: B = 0.772.
Interval [59.7, 62.4] captures uncertainty from the unknown between-group variance.
common_failure_modes: Using the raw observed mean (58.8) without shrinkage
Using only the grand mean (66.7) and ignoring group-specific data
Not recognizing that small-sample groups should be shrunk more toward the grand mean
Treating the between-group variance as known rather than uncertain
